verdict_reason: The dehī (embodied one) is mapped to the customer-requirement; the śarīra to implementation. Shankara's continuity-of-substrate reading appears in 'the customer-need walked through every wardrobe-transition essentially undamaged.' Ramanuja's affect-reframing reading appears in 'senior engineers eventually relinquish mourning over discarded codebases' (mature affect = wardrobe-change is ordinary). The two readings are presented as complementary in the disagreements_among_translators field, not conflated. No tradition is dropped. The doctrinally critical particle 'tathā' (in the same way) is preserved by the engineering analog's structural mirroring of the simile — wearer-unchanging-while-clothes-change maps onto requirement-unchanging-while-implementations-change. PASS.
